title: Within-Lap LambdaMART Urgency Specialist
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions against the field on the same lap, not just absolute thresholds.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions against the field on the same lap, not just absolute thresholds. XGBoost ranking objectives are explicitly designed for grouped ordering problems, so a lap-group ranker can learn which cars are most pit-urgent within each (Year,Race,LapNumber) slice and supply a complementary signal to the binary classifier.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost ranker with query groups set to (Year,Race,LapNumber), label 1 for PitNextLap and 0 otherwise. Use the ranker’s OOF score, within-group percentile, and top-k flags as meta-features or blend its test predictions with the base classifier.
expected_effect: Should capture relative urgency and opponent-context interactions that plain pointwise trees underuse.
risk: Validation must keep whole races isolated; otherwise same-race group structure can make the ranking signal look better locally than it generalizes.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
